value.prior_session_context: June 25 followed a mixed Asia session on June 24 (Hang Seng +0.3% at 23,412.18 snapping a five-day losing streak; KOSPI +3.26% at 8,471.02; Nikkei 225 -0.58% at 69,384 per TE news stream). The June 25 session was dominated by the after-the-bell Micron fiscal Q3 earnings beat from June 24 US trading, which catalyzed a region-wide chip-stock rally. Japan, South Korea, and mainland China benchmarks rose; Hong Kong and Australia fell.

market_session: Asia regular cash-equity sessions, Thursday 2026-06-25 (all closed)
text: Thursday, June 25, 2026 was a regular trading day for all referenced Asian cash-equity venues (Tokyo, Hong Kong, Shanghai, Shenzhen, Seoul, ASX), with no market holidays. The session was framed by the after-the-bell Micron Technology fiscal Q3 results from June 24 US trading, which beat sharply and catalyzed a region-wide chip-stock and AI-infrastructure rally. Japan, South Korea, and mainland China benchmarks rose sharply, while Hong Kong and Australia fell.

nikkei_225.basis: Official Nikkei close (Nikkei Inc. indexes portal); corroborated by Yahoo Finance historical and Trading Economics JP225 table. Previous close 69,174.97 (FRED, CNBC prior close)

kospi.basis: Trading Economics KOSPI dated table Jun/25: 8,930.30 / +459.28 / +5.42%. Previous close 8,471.02 (Jun/24)

hang_seng.basis: Yahoo Finance historical and Investing.com historical for Jun/25. Trading Economics news stream gives 23,077 / -335 / -1.4%. Lowest close since May 2025; 52-week low 22,978.59 (Jun/25/26 intraday, CNBC)

shanghai_composite.basis: Xinhua: 'Shanghai Composite Index up 0.23 percent to 4,120.28 points'; Trading Economics SHANGHAI table Jun/25: 4,120.28 / +0.23%

asx_200.basis: Trading Economics ASX200 CFD close Jun/25: 8,749.9 / -58.5 / -0.66%. News stream: 'slipped 60 points, or 0.7%, to close at 8,749.' Previous close 8,808.40

calculation.method: Nikkei 225 official close 72,366.34 vs prior 69,174.97; KOSPI 8,930.30 vs prior 8,471.02; Hang Seng 23,076.91 vs prior 23,412.18; Shanghai Composite 4,120.28 (Xinhua close); ASX 200 8,749.90 vs prior 8,808.40 (Trading Economics CFD close).

text: Asian equity indexes closed sharply mixed on Thursday, June 25, 2026. Japan's Nikkei 225 surged 3,191.37 points (+4.61%) to 72,366.34 and South Korea's KOSPI jumped 459.28 points (+5.42%) to 8,930.30, both led by chip-stock rallies on Micron's blowout earnings. Mainland China's Shanghai Composite added 0.23% to 4,120.28. Hong Kong's Hang Seng was the regional outlier, falling 335.27 points (-1.43%) to 23,076.91 — its lowest close since May 2025. Australia's ASX 200 slipped 58.50 points (-0.66%) to 8,749.90, a two-week low, as hawkish RBA commentary and weak gold miners weighed.

sk_hynix.basis: Trading Economics KOSPI news stream: 'SK hynix advanced sharply by ... 13.91%'; Reuters: 'closed higher at 13%'

samsung_electronics.basis: Trading Economics KOSPI news stream: 'Samsung Electronics ... 5.29%'

advantest.basis: Trading Economics JP225 component table Jun/25: 35,900 / +15.06%; news stream: 'Advantest (14.9%)'

tokyo_electron.basis: Trading Economics JP225 component table Jun/25: 75,340 / +7.78%; news stream: 'Tokyo Electron (8.1%)'

kioxia_holdings.basis: Trading Economics JP225 news stream: 'Kioxia Holdings (13.1%)'

taiyo_yuden.basis: Trading Economics JP225 news stream: 'Taiyo Yuden (11.3%)'

murata_manufacturing.basis: Trading Economics JP225 news stream: 'Murata Manufacturing (7.9%)'

value.catalyst: Micron Technology fiscal Q3 revenue $41.46B (vs $35.84B est), guidance ~$50B (vs $43.58B est); Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra: 'tight conditions to persist beyond calendar 2027'; $22B customer pre-pay commitments
value.interpretation: The chip-heavy Korean and Japanese benchmarks outperformed the region; the KOSPI +5.42% and Nikkei +4.61% were directly attributed by Trading Economics and Reuters to Micron-driven AI/memory demand optimism. SK hynix was additionally supported by its announced ~$29.4B (KRW 45.5T) Nasdaq ADR listing plan.

text: Asian chip and memory stocks surged on Micron's blowout earnings. South Korea's SK hynix closed +13.91% and Samsung Electronics +5.29%, lifted both by the Micron demand signal and by SK hynix's announced plan to raise up to KRW 45.5 trillion (~$29.4 billion) through a Nasdaq ADR listing. In Tokyo, the semiconductor test-equipment maker Advantest closed +15.06%, Tokyo Electron +7.78%, and unlisted-then-IPO'd Kioxia Holdings +13.1%, with Taiyo Yuden +11.3% and Murata Manufacturing +7.9%. J.P. Morgan recommended 'add on any dips' and maintained an overweight on South Korea; Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra said tight memory conditions should persist beyond calendar 2027. The chip rally was the single dominant force driving the KOSPI and Nikkei to their biggest one-day gains in the cycle.

hang_seng.basis: Yahoo Finance and Investing.com historical Jun/25; Trading Economics HK50 news stream: 'fell 335 points, or 1.4%, to close at 23,077'

context.southbound_inflows: Strong southbound inflows from mainland China supported Hong Kong equities amid IPO revival

losers.basis: Trading Economics HK50 component table Jun/25 and news stream

text: Hong Kong's Hang Seng was the regional outlier, falling 335.27 points (-1.43%) to 23,076.91 — its lowest close since May 2025 and a fresh 52-week-low intraday at 22,978.59. The slide came despite a chip-led regional risk-on tone, as investors remained cautious ahead of Hong Kong's trade balance data and as mega-cap internet and consumer names dragged: Tencent -1.73%, Meituan -2.44%, Xiaomi -2.87%, Sunny Optical Technology -11.7%, and Trip.com -10.9%. Strong southbound inflows from mainland China partly tempered losses. The divergence between the chip-driven Korea/Japan rallies and the Hong Kong underperformance was the session's defining cross-market structure.

shanghai_composite.basis: Xinhua: 'up 0.23 percent to 4,120.28 points'; Trading Economics SHANGHAI table Jun/25

chinent.basis: Xinhua: 'gained 2.84 percent to close at 4,371.99 points'

combined_turnover.basis: Xinhua: 'combined turnover of stocks covered by these two indices reached 3.59 trillion yuan (about 526.32 billion U.S. dollars), up from 3.28 trillion yuan on the previous trading day'

value.sector_leaders: Shares related to memory chips and ultracapacitors led the gains; the precious metal sector saw major declines (Xinhua)

text: Mainland Chinese benchmarks closed higher on Thursday. The Shanghai Composite added 0.23% to 4,120.28, the Shenzhen Component jumped 1.82% to 16,344.08, the ChiNext gained 2.84% to 4,371.99, and the STAR Composite rose 2.1% to 2,391.95. The CSI 300 closed at 5,020.10, up 1.56%. Combined turnover on the Shanghai and Shenzhen indices reached 3.59 trillion yuan (~$526.32 billion), up from 3.28 trillion yuan the prior session. Memory-chip and ultracapacitor shares led, while precious-metals stocks declined — the same memory-vs-gold pattern seen across the region.

usd_jpy.basis: Reuters global markets wrapup: 'The Japanese yen was near its lowest in 40 years against the dollar ... The yen was flat against the greenback at 161.80 per dollar.'

boj_rate.basis: Trading Economics Japan related indicators: Japan Interest Rate 1.00, previous 0.75, percent, reference Jun 2026

boj_commentary.speaker: BoJ board member Naoki Tamura
boj_commentary.stance: Hawkish — called for rate hikes every few months (Trading Economics news stream: 'BoJ Hawk Tamura Calls for Rate Hike Every Few Months')

market_session: Asia session Thursday 2026-06-25 (post-close FX quote from NY wrap)
text: The yen traded at 161.80 per dollar, flat on the day but near its lowest level in 40 years, with more intervention widely expected from Tokyo after the last bout around May did not stem the currency's decline. The Bank of Japan had raised its policy rate to 1.00% (from 0.75%), and BoJ board member Naoki Tamura — identified by Trading Economics as a hawk — called for rate hikes every few months. Japan's headline CPI was 1.50% year-over-year in May, up from 1.40% in April. The weak-yen backdrop supports the translation value of Japanese exporters' overseas earnings and was a tailwind for the Nikkei's chip-led rally, even as it raises intervention risk.

asx_200.basis: Trading Economics ASX200 CFD close Jun/25: 8,749.9 / -58.5 / -0.66%. News stream: 'slipped 60 points, or 0.7%, to close at 8,749'

macro_driver.employment: Stronger-than-expected rise in Australia's May employment
macro_driver.rba_commentary: Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser: inflation remains 'too high' and more work may be needed

losers.basis: Trading Economics ASX200 component table Jun/25 and news stream

text: Australia's ASX 200 slipped 58.50 points (-0.66%) to 8,749.90, a two-week low, as a stronger-than-expected May employment print reinforced expectations that the RBA will hold its hawkish stance, with Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser stressing that inflation remains 'too high.' Gold miners slid as bullion hit a seven-month low: Evolution Mining -3.9% and Northern Star Resources -3.1%. Energy names fell as oil retreated to pre-Iran-war levels: Woodside -3.3%, Santos -2.6%. Judo Capital plunged 39% after cutting its 2026 earnings outlook. The big four banks shed between 1.2% and 3.7%. Losses were partly capped by firmer US stock futures overnight.

brent_crude.basis: Reuters: 'Brent crude futures settled up 2% at $75.26 a barrel.' Context: pre-war levels as tankers pass through Hormuz

gold.basis: Reuters: 'Spot gold rose 0.64% to $4,026.09 an ounce.' Note: Asia-session gold miners sold off on bullion hitting a seven-month low (TE Australia wrap)

euro.basis: Reuters: 'The euro was last at $1.137, a whisker above Wednesday's 13-month low'

us_10y_yield.basis: Reuters: 'The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes fell 0.59 basis point to 4.394%'

value.interpretation: Easing oil prices amid US-Iran peace-talk progress and reopening Strait of Hormuz shipping were a key macro tailwind for oil-import-dependent Asia (especially Japan), helping risk appetite even as they weighed on Australian energy names. The weaker dollar and lower yields provided a supportive backdrop for regional equities, but gold's softness hit Australian gold miners.

market_session: NY close cross-asset reference for Asia session Thursday 2026-06-25
text: The cross-asset backdrop for the Asia session was supportive of risk appetite. Brent crude settled up 2% at $75.26 a barrel but remained near pre-US-Iran-war levels as tankers resumed passage through the Strait of Hormuz and peace-talk progress was signaled. The dollar index eased 0.15% to 101.45; the euro was at $1.137; the yen was flat at 161.80 but near 40-year lows. Spot gold rose 0.64% to $4,026.09 an ounce in NY trading, though Asian-session gold miners sold off on the weaker bullion tone. The US 10-year yield slipped to 4.394%. For oil-import-dependent Japan, the combination of a weaker yen (boosting exporter translations) and soft oil (easing import-cost pressure) was a double tailwind — even as the weak yen kept intervention risk on the radar.
